_comment: Endpoints intentionally not covered by the CLI. Each entry needs an endpoint (in OpenAPI-templated form, e.g. 'POST /admin/orgs/{org_id}/sudo') and a reason. Path-parameter names are normalized at match time, so the names you use here are documentation only. Categorize by reason prefix so we can review periodically: 'web-only-auth', 'webhook-receiver', 'public-endpoint', 'infra', 'meta', 'future-cli'. Maintained as part of S-103 in the integrations sprint plan.

deferred.reason: web-only-auth: web-app login flow; CLI uses bearer tokens directly
web-only-auth: web session refresh; CLI uses long-lived API keys
web-only-auth: email+password registration; CLI uses anonymous account upgrade flow instead
web-only-auth: email verification callback; CLI uses send-code/verify-code flow
web-only-auth: OAuth callback target; not initiated by CLI users
web-only-auth: OAuth redirect entry point; not initiated by CLI users
webhook-receiver: Stripe → MyAPI webhook; never called from CLI
webhook-receiver: Stripe confirmation callback; never called from CLI
webhook-receiver: SES → MyAPI email events; never called from CLI
webhook-receiver: user-configured inbound webhooks; receives traffic, not initiated by CLI users
infra: health check; not a user-facing capability
infra: ops/SRE status; not a user-facing capability
meta: the schema endpoint itself; the coverage tool consumes it but doesn't need a CLI command
meta: human-readable API docs page; browser-rendered, not a CLI command
public-endpoint: pixel/event ingest from published funnel pages; not a user-facing CLI capability
public-endpoint: form submission from published funnel pages; receives traffic
public-endpoint: signed/public asset URL access; consumers fetch directly, not via CLI
public-endpoint: pixel tracking redirect
public-endpoint: pixel tracking redirect
public-endpoint: shortened-URL redirect; resolved by browsers, not CLI
public-endpoint: direct-link unsubscribe (recipient clicks from email); browser target, not CLI
public-endpoint: RFC 8058 one-click unsubscribe (mail clients POST automatically); not CLI-driven
documentation-alias: REST-instinct alias for GET /image/orgs/{org_id}/jobs/{id} (already covered by `image get-job <id>`); same handler
openai-compat: drop-in for the OpenAI SDK — users set `base_url` and invoke via their OpenAI client, not the CLI
openai-compat: /v1 alias of chat/completions for SDKs that hard-code /v1; same as above
